name: Patrick Sandoval
reasoning: Sandoval remains in a closely managed workload since returning from a 25-month layoff/internal-brace surgery -- his 2026 debut was capped at 65 pitches over 4.1 IP for 5 K, and Boston's staff explicitly said they weren't going to let him approach a '95-pitch outing' this early back, which caps upside on total strikeouts even with strong per-inning stuff. That workload caution is consistent with the model's 4.7 trailing IP/start input, so I don't see reason to push innings higher. On the Toronto side, StatMuse data shows the Blue Jays have been one of the toughest lineups in MLB to strike out against left-handers this season, with a strikeout rate near 17-18.6% vs lefties, and the expected lineup vs. a lefty (Clement, Guerrero, Okamoto, Springer, Kirk, Varsho) is contact-oriented at the top with Clement hitting .296. That contact profile roughly matches the model's 18.6% opponent K-rate input and argues against expecting an outlier strikeout total. Given the short-leash workload management plus a contact-heavy Toronto lineup vs LHP, I'd shade slightly below the model's 4.2 projection to about 3.8, still comfortably above the 3.5 market line but with less margin than the raw K/9 trend alone would suggest.
